name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-3
title: scope-guard false positive: quoted-region stripping misses content spanning newlines in multi-line commands
description: extract_bash_targets in scope-guard.sh uses Python re.sub with pattern '"[^"]*"' to strip double-quoted regions before scanning for write targets. The character class [^"]*  does not match newlines by default (re.DOTALL is not set). When a Bash tool command uses backslash-newline line continuations, a --description "..." argument whose value spans multiple lines is not fully stripped. Content inside the quoted string that resembles a shell redirection is then matched as a write target. Observed: '--description "...text -> U+00F0..."' caused DENY for a fabricated path '/repo/U+00F0' because '->' was parsed as a redirect operator pointing at 'U+00F0'. Fix: add re.DOTALL to the double-quoted region stripping sub call so [^"]* matches across newlines, or pre-join continuation lines (strip backslash-newline sequences) before applying the regex.
